ROKU.title: Roku Rewinds Doubters With Ad-Fueled Earnings Beat

macro_events.event_summary: Trump publicly rejects current Iran deal terms, signaling continued/escalated tensions with Iran rather than near-term diplomatic resolution.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium on crude oil from potential supply disruption / sanctions enforcement
Increased likelihood of sustained or expanded Iran sanctions reducing global oil supply
Defense spending tailwind from heightened Middle East tensions

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major with direct leverage to higher Brent/WTI; every $5 oil move materially impacts upstream earnings. No Iranian production exposure to lose.
High-beta US shale pure-play (Permian); historically one of the strongest movers on Iran/Middle East risk headlines due to leveraged balance sheet and unhedged production.
Lockheed makes THAAD and PAC-3 missile defense systems deployed in Gulf states; sustained Iran tensions support FMS pipeline and supplemental defense appropriations.
Raytheon Patriot interceptors and Tomahawks are the direct munitions used/replenished in any Iran-linked conflict; replenishment contracts already elevated.
Prime on Global Hawk ISR and B-21; Middle East ISR demand and strategic deterrence narrative strengthen on prolonged standoff.
Diamondback is a Permian pure-play with ~100% US onshore exposure; benefits from oil price spike without geopolitical operational risk.

losers.rationale: Delta has the lowest fuel hedge ratio among US legacies; jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs and tracks crude with a lag. Direct margin compression on oil spike.
United similarly unhedged on fuel; international long-haul routes also exposed to Middle East airspace rerouting costs if tensions escalate.
Southwest historically hedged but current book is light; single-fleet 737 operator with no fuel cost diversification, high sensitivity to crude.
Carnival bunker fuel costs rise with crude; Mediterranean/Middle East itineraries face rerouting risk if Strait of Hormuz tensions escalate.
FedEx Express jet fuel is a major cost line; Middle East hub in Dubai exposes operations to regional escalation risk.

headlines: Trump says 'Iran wants to make a deal, but I'm not satisfied with it'

macro_events.event_summary: Oil prices fall as traders interpret Iran's signals as moving toward ending hostilities, easing geopolitical risk premium.
primary_drivers: Reduced geopolitical risk premium in crude
Lower input/fuel costs for transport and consumer-facing businesses
Compressed margins for upstream E&P producers

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of airline operating costs; falling crude directly expands margins. Delta has historically been one of the most fuel-cost-leveraged majors with limited hedging.
Same jet fuel cost mechanism as DAL; United runs minimal fuel hedges so spot crude drop flows directly to margin.
Southwest's fuel costs are a major P&L line; lower crude reduces breakeven load factor and supports near-term EPS estimates.
Diesel and jet fuel are key cost inputs for ground and Express segments; lower oil reduces fuel surcharge drag and supports margin.
Naphtha/ethane feedstock costs track crude; lower oil widens petrochemical spreads for an integrated chemicals producer.

losers.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream exposure; lower crude directly compresses E&P realizations and segment EBITDA.
Pure-play US oil-weighted E&P with high operating leverage to crude prices; cash flow falls roughly linearly with WTI.
Upstream-only major with no downstream offset, so falling crude hits earnings without refining margin cushion.
Oilfield services demand and pricing power tied to E&P capex, which gets cut when crude falls and geopolitical premium evaporates.
International-weighted services franchise particularly exposed to Middle East activity; an Iran de-escalation reduces both prices and regional spending intensity.
Permian pure-play with high crude-price beta; lower oil pressures buyback capacity and variable dividend.
Smaller-cap E&P with higher debt and operating leverage; among the most crude-sensitive names on the down move.

headlines: Oil Falls as Traders Parse Iran’s Stance to End War
